pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 107, 181-202: File created in SRC_DIR/DoseCalculator.java where SRC_DIR=$PROJECT_DIR/src/main/java/com/med/physics
setup_task.sh lines 93-104, 167-178: File created in SRC_DIR/CalibrationConstants.java where SRC_DIR=$PROJECT_DIR/src/main/java/com/med/physics
setup_task.sh line 175: public static final double LINEAR_ACCELERATOR_FACTOR = 1.035;

privileged_info_summary: The task applies a physics bug fix patch to a RadiationTherapy Eclipse project. The patch contains two critical fixes: (1) DoseCalculator.java is corrected with Inverse Square Law logic (Math.pow(distanceSourceToTumor / 100.0, 2)) to properly calculate Monitor Units for varying source-to-target distances; (2) CalibrationConstants.java updates LINEAR_ACCELERATOR_FACTOR from 1.020 to 1.035 based on new calibration data (2023-10-25). The verification test expects calculated Monitor Units of 237.377 (±0.1) for a 200 cGy dose at 110cm distance, which validates both fixes work correctly. All files are located in a Maven-configured RadiationTherapy project in /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/, with the patch file at /home/ga/Desktop/physics_fix.patch.
